events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known as Ringo Starr, the iconic drummer for The Beatles, was born on this day in 1940.

angles.name: Drummer Legend
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! The man who kept the beat for the biggest band in the world.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, songwriter, and astrophysicist, Sir Brian May, celebrates his 79th birthday today.

angles.name: Guitar Hero
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 79th birthday to the one and only Brian May! From Queen anthems to astrophysics, what an incredible life.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, the iconic frontman of The Rolling Stones, was born on this day in 1943.

angles.name: Rock Icon
angles.copy: Still got the moves! Happy 83rd birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger. What's your favourite Stones track?

events.title: MTV Launched
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched in the US on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being its very first music video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. The irony is not lost on us, but it certainly changed music forever!

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, featuring iconic tracks like 'Supersonic' and 'Live Forever'.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: 22 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed the sound of British music. Still sounds as fresh as ever!

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in counterculture history, took place from August 15-18, 1969.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: Imagine being at Woodstock '69. Three days of peace, love, and music that defined a generation. What a lineup!

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's Death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the passing of the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who died on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley on the anniversary of his passing. His music continues to influence generations. Long live The King.

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's Death
events.blurb: Tupac Shakur, one of the most influential rappers of all time, was tragically shot in Las Vegas on September 7, 1996, and died on September 13.

angles.name: Tupac Remembered
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur. A voice that shaped a generation and left an undeniable legacy on music.

events.title: Anniversary of Jimi Hendrix's Death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the death of guitar virtuoso Jimi Hendrix, who passed away on September 18, 1970.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Remembering the unparalleled genius of Jimi Hendrix on the anniversary of his passing. Still inspiring guitarists worldwide.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on September 26, 1969, featuring the famous zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: One of the most iconic album covers and tracklists ever. The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' was released this day in 1969. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, the American-British singer, songwriter, and founding member of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! A true rock icon and the powerhouse voice of The Pretenders.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, the distinctive frontman of Oasis and a successful solo artist, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Rock 'n' Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that undeniable rock 'n' roll swagger. As you were.

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine
events.blurb: John Lennon released his timeless anthem for peace, 'Imagine', as a single on October 11, 1971, from the album of the same name.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: 55 years since John Lennon gave the world 'Imagine'. A song that continues to resonate with its powerful message of peace and unity.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: Music in Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? 25 years ago today, Apple launched the device that put '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changed music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first 007 film, Dr. No, which premiered on October 5, 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Raise a martini (shaken, not stirred) to the world's most famous secret agent. What's your favourite Bond film?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles and an iconic solo artist, was born on this day in 1940.

angles.name: Remembering Lennon
angles.copy: On what would have been his 86th birthday, we remember the incredible life and enduring legacy of John Lennon. Imagine.
